headlines.title: Is Trump any closer to an Iran exit strategy?
headlines.description: The White House insists the president is dictating events, but even some Republicans are frustrated by mixed messaging about his war aims.

headlines.title: National Savings and Investments bank set to pay millions to customers over misplaced funds
headlines.description: The government-backed bank has been accused of a series of errors, including not paying bereaved families money that was rightfully theirs.

headlines.title: Meta and YouTube found liable in landmark social media addiction trial
headlines.description: A woman has been awarded $6m in a verdict that could have implications for hundreds of other cases in the US.

headlines.title: Chris Mason: How will the UK respond to US court verdict on social media?
headlines.description: Will the decision of a Los Angeles jury have an impact on the UK government as it considers potential future regulations on social media, writes the BBC's political editor.

headlines.title: British forces will be able to board Russian shadow fleet ships in UK waters
headlines.description: The move will "starve Putin's war machine", Starmer says as he arrives in Finland for a military summit.

headlines.title: Prepare for turbulence - how a prolonged Middle East conflict could reshape how we fly
headlines.description: The Gulf's hub airports made long-distance travel cheaper - but now their future looks unclear.

headlines.title: Harry Potter TV show trailer: Everything we know so far
headlines.description: The HBO reboot, which is due in 2027, is a spin-off from the original books and the film franchise.

headlines.title: Men released on bail over Jewish charity ambulance arson attack
headlines.description: Four Jewish charity-owned ambulances were set alight in north London on Monday.

headlines.title: 'I have no recollection of anything' - Epstein survivor says she was drugged and raped
headlines.description: A survivor of Epstein's abuses spoke publicly for the first time to BBC Newsnight about her experience of being raped and drugged by the sex offender.

headlines.title: Parents should monitor children '24/7' on Roblox, says developer
headlines.description: Roblox said safety was a top priority and it had advanced safeguards in place to keep users safe.

headlines.title: 'Books make life better' - Camilla's love of reading explored in BBC documentary
headlines.description: The Queen will reflect on the childhood experiences that sparked her passion for reading and reflect on the comfort she still finds in books.

headlines.title: Iran's rejection of US talks reflects deep mistrust
headlines.description: This does not mean the door is shut, as Iran's tough stance may be more about setting conditions than rejecting diplomacy.

headlines.title: Who wants what and why from US-Iran peace talks?
headlines.description: There is indirect contact and channels between the two sides - but a deal may still be a long way off.

headlines.title: Iranians mark their New Year with anger, fear, and defiance
headlines.description: Cleaning her home for the festival, one Iranian told us: "Perhaps this dark night will finally give way to dawn."

headlines.title: How the conflict in the Middle East is causing fuel protests in the Philippines
headlines.description: BBC News Asia Business Correspondent Suranajana Tewari spoke to people taking to the streets of Manila.

headlines.title: Twenty years ago, Scotland banned smoking in pubs. Did it save lives?
headlines.description: Exactly 20 years ago, Scotland became the first part of the UK to outlaw smoking in bars, restaurants and offices.

headlines.title: Octopus boss: We've seen a 50% rise in solar panel sales since start of Iran war
headlines.description: The UK giant is optimistic but chief executive Greg Jackson tells the BBC he is making contingency plans.

headlines.title: I re-visited one of England's most spectacular hikes after a £5.5m upgrade
headlines.description: The long-distance Coast to Coast route which stretches across the Lake District, Yorkshire Dales and North York Moors is now an official National Trail.

headlines.title: In one of country's poorest states, Venezuelans hope for post-Maduro boom
headlines.description: Ione Wells travels to Sucre, where a lack of investment has hampered locals' prospects.

headlines.title: 'Managing my finances with ADHD felt overwhelming'
headlines.description: ADHD affects the brain's frontal lobe and can impact organisation and focus, a specialist says.

headlines.title: Rap legend Slick Rick on Mobo honour and shaping hip-hop: 'We took novels to the next level'
headlines.description: The British-born rapper looks back as he's awarded a lifetime achievement prize at the Mobo Awards.

headlines.title: The Papers: 'Iran rejects peace plan' and '£400m cost of savings scandal'
headlines.description: Iran's rejection of US talks and Donald Trump's reaction features on Thursday's front pages.

headlines.title: Dogs became man's best friend far earlier than thought, scientists find
headlines.description: A jawbone found in a Somerset cave rewrites the story of when and how dogs became our best friends.

headlines.title: Singer Duffy to share sex assault ordeal in documentary
headlines.description: Disney+ says it has been given "unprecedented access" to the singer in her first major interview in 15 years.

headlines.title: CO2 plant in UK to reopen in war contingency plan
headlines.description: The government will invest £100m to restart the Teesside site producing carbon dioxide, a key part of food and drink manufacturing.

headlines.title: Rocket stocks soar on report Musk's SpaceX to file for share sale
headlines.description: Reports it plans the biggest listing ever sent the shares of firms in its orbit soaring in US trade on Wednesday.

headlines.title: Australia's sunscreen regulator wants new rules after recent product scandal
headlines.description: The medicines regulator is proposing stricter rules after more than a dozen products were recalled.

headlines.title: Premier League star caught speeding after training
headlines.description: The former Manchester United player is fined for speeding as he left the club's training ground.

headlines.title: Ministers hope red tape cuts will speed up decision-making
headlines.description: The Conservatives said the changes were being made by a government "stuck in consultation paralysis".

headlines.description: Are the US and Iran peace talks going anywhere?

headlines.title: Bale left big boots to fill - will Wilson fire Wales to World Cup?
headlines.description: Nobody can replace the great Gareth Bale, but Harry Wilson is stepping up more than anyone as Wales aim to qualify for the World Cup.

headlines.title: Man Utd down but not out - Skinner sure Bayern lead can be overturned

headlines.title: Johnson to give back alleged 'secret' payment of $500,000

headlines.title: Will there be an upset? Northern Ireland plan to stun Italy
headlines.description: Manager Michael O'Neill says Northern Ireland's young side will play without fear in Thursday's World Cup play-off against Italy.

headlines.title: Man Utd come from behind twice but lose first leg to Bayern
headlines.description: Manchester United fan Pernille Harder scored twice against her childhood club at Old Trafford as Bayern Munich gained a precious first-leg advantage in their Champions League quarter-final tie.

headlines.title: Is Saka's form becoming a worry for Arsenal?

headlines.title: From Harvard to Sale - Erica Jarrell-Searcy's epic PWR pilgrimage
headlines.description: A chance email in freshers' week at Harvard set Erica Jarrell-Searcy on a journey to Sale Sharks.
